mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that depress S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across durable and non-durable consumer demand, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series deviates from its 12-month moving average by more than 1 standard deviation, or Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects immediate capacity utilization and supply-side recovery, generating short-term positive momentum in equity markets as investors price in near-term earnings revisions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of periods following industrial production acceleration during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 235 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that erode real corporate margins and force anticipatory monetary tightening, resulting in negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 235 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations when payroll growth exceeds 200k but unemployment remains flat.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic output, which translates into immediate positive sentiment and short-term upward price pressure on broad equity indices.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with quarterly GDP revisions over a 5-year sample, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the index of industrial production signal a supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output and earnings expectations, leading to negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production.
